narration: You've got two stocks — one up forty percent, one down forty percent. Which one do you sell first? I'll tell you what your brain picks, and it's not the right answer.
visual: Two SVG stock chart lines side by side — rising green line labeled '+40%' on left, falling red line labeled '-40%' on right. Person silhouette stands between them looking toward the losing chart.

narration: Almost every investor sells the winner and holds the loser. Behavioral economists call this the Disposition Effect, and it's one of the most expensive mistakes in all of investing.
visual: Simplified brain SVG. Two arrows spring in — triangle pointing up labeled 'SELL WINNERS' in green, triangle pointing down labeled 'HOLD LOSERS' in red. Red badge: 'THE EXPENSIVE MISTAKE'.

narration: Studies show you're one-point-seven times more likely to sell a winning stock than a losing one. That single habit costs the average retail investor one-point-five percent per year in returns.
visual: Two growing bar charts side by side — shorter red bar labeled 'YOUR RETURNS +8.5%' vs taller green bar labeled 'S&P 500 +10%'. Large '1.7x' stat springs in at the bottom.

narration: Your brain literally refuses to lock in a loss. It's called loss aversion — losses feel two times more painful than gains feel good, so you freeze and hope it bounces back.
visual: Two weighted boxes side by side — smaller green box labeled '+$1,000 GAIN', larger red box twice the height labeled '-$1,000 LOSS'. '2x HEAVIER' label between them. Red 'LOSS AVERSION' badge below.

narration: Here's the brutal truth: over thirty years, that one-point-five percent annual drag compounds into forty-seven thousand dollars in missing wealth. That's the real price of holding your losers.
visual: Two animated lines drawing across a time axis from Year 1 to Year 30 — solid green 'MARKET' line rising steeply, dashed red 'YOU' line rising more slowly. '$47,000' callout springs in showing the gap.

narration: The fix is dead simple: set your stop-loss rule before you buy, never after. Fifteen percent max loss, then move on. The money you don't lose is the money you keep.
visual: Notepad SVG with handwritten-style rules: 'STOP LOSS: -15% — set BEFORE buying'. Checkmark springs in. CTA text fades up: 'Pre-set exit rules keep $47,000 in your pocket.'
